mission.title: kubeflow: Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow
mission.description: Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow. Requested by 4+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubeflow deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubeflow version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
helm list -n kubeflow 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubeflow installation.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
There's a lot of good jupyter images already out there.

For example #767 discusses a Jupyter image with swift support for TensorFlow.

We'd also like to support images with R.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow
steps.description: Modified entrypoint command for jupyter images to launch jupyter on boot. --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/kubeflow/kubeflow/2517) ```yaml container.Env = append(container.Env, corev1.EnvVar{ Name: "NB_PREFIX", Value: "/notebook/" + instance.Namespace + "/" + instance.Name, }) ```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Modified entrypoint command for jupyter images to launch jupyter on boot. --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/kubeflow/kubeflow/2517)
codeSnippets:
# allow apache2 to use the NB_PREFIX provided by kubeflow
# note: we strip off any trailing / for consistency
shopt -s extglob;
echo "export NB_PREFIX_NO_SLASH=${NB_PREFIX%%+(/)}" >> /etc/apache2/envvars

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
